reforms.description: A new university law passed in April 2007 strengthens quality assessment of university education, widens universities’ autonomy over course content, and introduces modular course structures to encourage student mobility. Additionally, a new law has been approved that facilitates the management of public research centres, granting them more autonomy and improving their budgetary, managerial, and administrative independence.
reforms.component_name: University Law and Research Centre Management
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing university autonomy and quality assessment is expected to improve educational outcomes and research quality, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The university law was passed and the management law was approved, indicating they are in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes in the management and quality of higher education and public research.
reforms.source_quote: A new university law, passed in April 2007, strengthens quality assessment of university education, widens universities’ autonomy over the content of their courses and introduces modular course structures.

reforms.description: Consideration should be given to creating a national funding scheme to reward the creation of centres of excellence in university education, supplementing existing regional funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Establishing a national funding scheme would incentivize the development of high-quality educational institutions, contributing to a more skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The text indicates that the creation of the funding scheme is under consideration.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the proposal is still in consideration and not yet implemented.
reforms.source_quote: Consideration should be given to creating a national funding scheme, supplementing existing regional funding, to reward the creation of centres of excellence in university education.

reforms.description: The government intends to significantly increase researchers’ remuneration and base their recruitment on evaluation to improve the quality of public sector research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing remuneration and improving recruitment practices are expected to attract and retain high-quality researchers, enhancing the overall research output.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced intentions to implement these changes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to improve the quality of public sector research but is not as comprehensive as major structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: The government intends to significantly increase researchers’ remuneration and basing their recruitment to a greater degree on evaluation.

reforms.description: A programme was launched to evaluate all R&D initiatives in the INGENIO programme, aimed at assessing incentives for private research and innovation expenditure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Evaluating R&D initiatives can lead to better-targeted incentives, potentially increasing private sector investment in research and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been launched and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The evaluation programme is significant for improving R&D initiatives but does not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: A programme was launched to evaluate all R&D initiatives in the INGENIO programme.
